title: First Read-Only Generated Adapter
goal: Implement #359 by generating or mechanically assembling one read-only CLI adapter for root `agentic-workspace defaults` from authoritative command-adapter contracts while preserving current behavior.
non_goals: Migrate planning or memory package CLIs.

intent.outcome: The `defaults` CLI adapter wiring is generated or mechanically assembled from command_adapter_generation.json instead of being a hand-authored command branch.
intent.constraints: Stay on master; keep runtime primitive implementation hand-written; preserve user-facing behavior; keep generated surfaces clearly sourced.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest generator/assembler and generated output shape that proves the boundary.
intent.escalation: Stop before broad CLI refactors, lifecycle commands, or package CLI migration.

execution.next_step: Archive this slice and continue with #360 generated-adapter drift/conformance guardrails.
execution.proof: Use proof --changed, contract checks, focused CLI/conformance tests, ruff, planning checks, reconcile, summary.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Generated shipped CLI adapters from authoritative package definitions.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After the first generated read-only adapter lands and needs stronger drift/conformance guardrails.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: A first shipped read-only command can be wired from generated adapter metadata.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Full drift guardrails, no-direct-edit enforcement, package CLI extension, and multi-adapter projection requirements.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The first generated adapter path needs stronger drift guardrails before more commands are migrated; #360 remains the continuation.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Generated defaults adapter metadata is current, the root CLI dispatches through generated adapter metadata, and focused tests preserve defaults behavior.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: #360 should harden broader conformance and no-direct-edit drift checks before expanding generated adapters.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #360 conformance and drift checks for generated CLI adapters.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Continue implementing planned work until done.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Proceed from #358 into #359 using root `defaults` as the first generated adapter candidate.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Generate/mechanically assemble defaults command adapter wiring from command_adapter_generation.json.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check that parser/help/dispatch metadata comes from the contract and runtime behavior stays hand-written.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: CLI adapter generation script/output, root CLI dispatch glue, command adapter contract/checks/tests, planning state/evidence/review artifacts.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: proof --changed; check_contract_tooling_surfaces; generated adapter freshness check; workspace CLI tests; generated conformance tests; ruff; planning checks; reconcile; summary.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any broad CLI dispatch rewrite beyond generated adapter hook and defaults adapter.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Lifecycle commands, planning/memory package CLIs, generated adapter guardrail policy beyond focused freshness check.

stop_conditions.stop when: #359 is closed, committed, pushed, and #360 remains planned.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The first adapter cannot be generated without rewriting large CLI dispatch.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Generated adapter behavior or freshness checks fail.

context_budget.live working set: #359, #358 contract, src/agentic_workspace/cli.py defaults dispatch, tests/test_workspace_cli.py, tests/test_generated_tool_conformance.py
context_budget.recoverable later: Full generated adapter lane details and package CLI migration
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run and closeout_distillation before archive.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before broad CLI dispatch refactor or generator generalization.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Generate the first read-only CLI adapter slice.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: One read-only command; no lifecycle; runtime primitives stay hand-written.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact generator file name, generated module shape, and focused tests.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The change needs broad dispatch architecture or package CLI migration.

immediate_next_action: Inspect current defaults dispatch and add generated adapter metadata plus dispatch hook.
invariants: Do not move runtime primitive behavior into contracts.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Generated adapter metadata may own command dispatch wiring; runtime operation functions still own live behavior.
open_questions_to_close: What generated output shape is the smallest useful proof?
What check proves the generated output matches command_adapter_generation.json?
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_generated_tool_conformance.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py
uv run ruff check src tests scripts
completion_criteria: `defaults` adapter wiring is generated or mechanically assembled from command_adapter_generation.json.
Focused tests prove generated adapter path.
No active execplan remains after archive closeout.

execution_run.what happened: Generated src/agentic_workspace/generated_command_adapters.py from command_adapter_generation.json, routed root defaults dispatch through generated metadata, preserved hand-written runtime behavior, and added freshness plus dispatch tests.
execution_run.scope touched: Root CLI generated adapter metadata and dispatch glue, command-adapter contract status, generator script, focused tests, planning state/evidence/review artifacts.
execution_run.changed surfaces: cli.py, command_adapter_generation.json, generated_command_adapters.py, generate_command_adapters.py, tests, planning state/evidence/review.
execution_run.validations run: proof --changed; check_contract_tooling_surfaces; generate_command_adapters --check; pytest test_workspace_cli/test_generated_tool_conformance/test_contract_tooling; ruff; planning doctor; check_planning_surfaces; reconcile; summary.
execution_run.result for continuation: #359 closed; #360 remains open for broader generated-adapter drift/conformance guardrails.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; only one read-only root command adapter path was generated and runtime primitives stayed hand-written.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes for #359; no for parent #356, which continues through #360-#363.
finished_run_review.config compliance: dogfooded preflight, proof, summary, reconcile, doctor, and planning closeout.

proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: 170 focused CLI/contract tests passed, 7 generated conformance/contract tests passed, ruff passed, planning doctor/check/reconcile/summary clean.

system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can ordinary defaults interface changes move through contracts/generated adapter metadata rather than cli.py?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #359 delivered the first read-only generated adapter proof; parent #356 still needs drift guardrails and package adapter extension.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen #359 if defaults cannot be maintained through generated adapter metadata without bespoke command dispatch.
